content:
# Mark Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 40v–41r (PDF pages 81–82) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_015_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_015_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And immediately in-the-morning, the chief-priests having-made a-council with the elders and scribes and the whole Sanhedrin, having-bound Jesus, led-[him]-away and delivered [him] to Pilate.

² And Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of-the Jews?" And he, answering him, says, "You say [so]."

³ And the chief-priests were-accusing him of-many-[things].

⁴ And Pilate again was-asking him, saying, "Do-you-answer nothing? See how-many-things they-accuse you-of."

⁵ But Jesus no-longer answered anything, so-that Pilate was-marveling.

⁶ Now at [each] feast he-was-releasing to-them one prisoner whom they-were-requesting.

⁷ And there-was the-one called Barabbas, having-been-bound with the insurrectionists who in the insurrection had-committed murder.

⁸ And the crowd, having-gone-up, began to-ask [him to do] just-as he-was-doing for-them.

⁹ And Pilate answered them, saying, "Do-you-want [that] I-release to-you the King of-the Jews?"

¹⁰ For he-knew that because-of envy the chief-priests had-delivered him.

¹¹ But the chief-priests stirred-up the crowd so-that he-might rather release Barabbas to-them.

¹² And Pilate again answering was-saying to-them, "What then do-you-want me-to-do [with him] whom you-call the King of-the Jews?"

¹³ And they again cried-out, "Crucify him!"

¹⁴ And Pilate was-saying to-them, "For what evil did-he-do?" But they cried-out exceedingly, "Crucify him!"

¹⁵ And Pilate, wanting to-satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas to-them. And having-flogged Jesus, he-delivered [him] in-order-that he-might-be-crucified.

¹⁶ And the soldiers led him away inside the courtyard — which is [the] Praetorium — and they-call-together the whole cohort.

¹⁷ And they-dress him [in] purple, and having-woven a-crown of-thorns, they-place [it] on him.

¹⁸ And they-began to-salute him, "Hail, King of-the Jews!"

¹⁹ And they-were-striking his head with a-reed and were-spitting on him, and bending the knees, were-worshiping him.

²⁰ And when they-mocked him, they-stripped him of-the purple and dressed him [in] his own garments. And they-lead him out in-order-that they-might-crucify him.

²¹ And they-compel a-certain passer-by, Simon of-Cyrene, coming from the field — the father of Alexander and Rufus — that he-might-carry his cross.

²² And they-bring him to the place Golgotha, which is, being-translated, Place of-a-Skull.

²³ And they-were-giving him wine mixed-with-myrrh, but he did not take [it].

²⁴ And they-crucify him, and they-divide his garments, casting lots upon them [to determine] who takes what.

²⁵ And it-was the third hour, and they-crucified him.

²⁶ And the inscription of his charge was-written-above: THE KING OF THE JEWS.

²⁷ And with him they-crucify two robbers, one at [his] right and one at his left.

²⁸ [And the Scripture was fulfilled that says, "He was counted with the lawless."]

²⁹ And those passing-by were-blaspheming him, shaking their heads and saying, "Ha! The-one destroying the temple and building [it] in three days —

³⁰ save yourself, having-come-down from the cross!"

³¹ Likewise also the chief-priests, mocking among themselves with the scribes, were-saying, "He-saved others; himself he-is not able to-save.

³² Let the Christ, the King of Israel, come-down now from the cross, in-order-that we-might-see and believe." And those having-been-crucified-with him were-reviling him.

³³ And when the sixth hour came, darkness came upon the whole land until the ninth hour.

³⁴ And at the ninth hour, Jesus cried with a-loud voice, "Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?" — which is, being-translated, "My God, my God, why have-you-forsaken me?"

³⁵ And some of-those standing-by, having-heard, were-saying, "Look, he-calls Elijah."

³⁶ And someone, having-run and having-filled a-sponge with sour-wine, having-placed [it] on a-reed, was-giving-him-to-drink, saying, "Leave [him], let-us-see if Elijah comes to-take him-down."

³⁷ But Jesus, having-let-out a-loud cry, expired.

³⁸ And the curtain of-the temple was-torn in two from top to bottom.

³⁹ And the centurion who had-stood opposite him, having-seen that he thus expired, said, "Truly this man was God's Son."

⁴⁰ And there-were also women observing from-afar, among whom [were] both Mary the Magdalene and Mary the [mother] of James the less and of Joses, and Salome,

⁴¹ who when he-was in Galilee were-following him and were-serving him, and many other-[women] who had-come-up-with him to Jerusalem.

⁴² And already evening having-come, since it-was the Day-of-Preparation — which is [the day] before the Sabbath —

⁴³ Joseph from Arimathea, a-respected council-member, who also himself was-awaiting the kingdom of God, having-dared, went-in to Pilate and asked-for the body of Jesus.

⁴⁴ And Pilate was-surprised that he-had already died. And having-summoned the centurion, he-asked him if he-had already died.

⁴⁵ And having-learned [this] from the centurion, he-granted the corpse to Joseph.

⁴⁶ And having-bought a-linen-cloth, having-taken him-down, he-wrapped [him] in the linen-cloth and placed him in a-tomb which had-been-hewn out-of rock, and he-rolled a-stone against the door of-the tomb.

⁴⁷ And Mary the Magdalene and Mary the [mother] of Joses were-observing where he-has-been-placed.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **15:25 — "the third hour":** 9:00 AM. John 19:14 says "about the sixth hour" (noon). This is one of the most famous chronological discrepancies in the Gospels. I follow Mark/Sinaiticus without harmonization.

2. **15:28 — Bracketed verse:** Absent from Sinaiticus and Vaticanus. This is a later scribal addition quoting Isaiah 53:12. I bracket it as textually secondary.

3. **15:34 — "Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΛΩΙ ΕΛΩΙ ΛΕΜΑ ΣΑΒΑΧΘΑΝΙ — Aramaic (with the Aramaic form Eloi rather than the Hebrew Eli of Matthew 27:46). This is Psalm 22:1. The cry is quoted in the original language and then translated for the Greek audience.

4. **15:39 — "God's Son" (ΘΕΟΥ ΥΙΟΣ):** The Greek lacks the definite article — literally "a son of God" or "God's son." Whether the centurion intends the full Christian confession or merely a pagan acknowledgment ("a divine man") is deliberately ambiguous.

5. **15:44 — "corpse" (ΠΤΩΜΑ):** Mark shifts from ΣΩΜΑ ("body," v. 43) to ΠΤΩΜΑ ("corpse," v. 45) — a stark, clinical word. Jesus is now a dead body.

6. **15:46 — "linen cloth" (ΣΙΝΔΟΝΑ):** The same word used for the young man's garment in 14:51-52. Whether this verbal echo is intentional is debated.

— EMET, AI Translator
